item.description: An American fighter jet attacked an Iranian tanker in the Gulf of Oman as it tried to break through the U.S. military blockade and make its way to an Iranian port, according to the U.S. Central Command. It comes as Iran says it’s deliberating a response to what the Wall Street Journal describes as a one page, 14-point U.S. proposal to end the war. NBC’s Richard Engel reports for TODAY from Jerusalem.

media:content.media:title: Iran Says Its Reviewing 14-Point US Proposal to End the War
media:content.media:description: An American fighter jet attacked an Iranian tanker in the Gulf of Oman as it tried to break through the U.S. military blockade and make its way to an Iranian port, according to the U.S. Central Command. It comes as Iran says it’s deliberating a response to what the Wall Street Journal describes as a one page, 14-point U.S. proposal to end the war. NBC’s Richard Engel reports for TODAY from Jerusalem.

item.title: Iran Says Its Reviewing 14-Point US Proposal to End the War

item.dc:creator: Yuliya Talmazan
Mosheh Gains
Courtney Kube
item.description: Ships in the Strait of Hormuz might be the only things that aren’t moving

media:content.media:text: APTOPIX Iran War Strait of Hormuz
media:content.media:description: A container ship sits at anchor as a small motorboat passes in the foreground in the Strait of Hormuz off Bandar Abbas, Iran, Saturday, May 2, 2026.

item.title: Why the Strait of Hormuz will take a long time to reboot

item.dc:creator: Jonathan Allen
Natasha Korecki
item.description: Amid turmoil over the party’s future, Kamala Harris has been privately sounding out confidants about her own prospects.

media:content.media:description: Kamala Harris, former Vice President and 2024 Presidential candidate, arrives during the National Action Network (NAN) Convention in New York on April 10, 2026.

media:content.media:text: Kamala Harris Book Tour In Los Angeles
media:content.media:description: Harris spoke in Los Angeles in September for her book tour.

media:content.media:text: California Gov. Newsom Attends A "Yes On Prop 50" Volunteer Event In L.A.
media:content.media:description: Harris spoke at a "Yes on Prop 50" volunteer event at the Los Angeles Convention Center in November.

item.title: Kamala Harris wants the DNC to release its autopsy report of the 2024 campaign

item.dc:creator: Jane C. Timm
item.description: Three state senators who were defeated in Tuesday’s primary told NBC News they haven’t changed their minds about the redistricting vote that drew Trump’s ire.

media:content.media:text: Indiana state Sen. Greg Walker, recites the Pledge of Allegiance
media:content.media:description: Indiana state Sen. Greg Walker, recites the Pledge of Allegiance on Tuesday, Nov. 18, 2025, during Org Day, the ceremonial first day of the 2026 legislative session, at the Indiana Statehouse in Indianapolis.

media:content.media:text: Indiana state Sen. Jim Buck, R-Kokomo, gives a concession speech Tuesday, May 5, 2026, during Howard County GOP's election night
media:content.media:description: Indiana state Sen. Jim Buck concedes in Kokomo after losing his primary Tuesday.

media:content.media:text: Indiana Senate Votes Down Trump-Backed Congressional Map
media:content.media:description: Republicans hoped a new congressional map in Indiana would have given them control over all nine districts.

item.title: Indiana Republicans who lost their jobs after bucking Trump have ‘zero regrets’

item.dc:creator: Bridget Bowman
item.description: Democrat Chedrick Greene has won a special state Senate election in Michigan, NBC News projects, ensuring his party will keep control of the closely divided chamber in a key battleground state

media:content.media:description: Democrat Chedrick Greene defeated Republican Jason Tunney in a special election for a Michigan state Senate seat.
Democrat Chedrick Greene defeated Republican Jason Tunney in a special election for a Michigan state Senate seat.

item.title: Democrats keep control of Michigan state Senate after special election win

item.dc:creator: Henry J. Gomez
item.description: Businessman Vivek Ramaswamy has won Ohio’s Republican primary for governor, NBC News projects, advancing to what could be an expensive and competitive general election with Democrat Amy Acton in a state that has been brutal for Democrats in recent elections.

media:content.media:description: Republican Ohio gubernatorial candidate Vivek speaks during a watch party at the Spruce St. Sporting sports bar after winning the party's nomination for governor Tuesday, May 5, 2026, in Columbus.

item.title: Vivek Ramaswamy wins GOP nomination for governor in Ohio

item.description: Video shows the captain of a cruise ship affected by a hantavirus outbreak informing passengers on April 12 that one person had died on board, saying the death was believed to be due to natural causes and was not infectious.

media:content.media:title: Video shows captain reporting first death on cruise ship affected by hantavirus
media:content.media:description: Video shows the captain of a cruise ship affected by a hantavirus outbreak informing passengers on April 12 that one person had died on board, saying the death was believed to be due to natural causes and was not infectious.

item.title: Video shows captain reporting first death on cruise ship affected by hantavirus

item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Chuck
Matt Lavietes
item.description: Thomas Shaknovsky said in a deposition in November that he believed he was removing 70-year-old William Bryan’s spleen, not his liver.

media:content.media:description: Officers with the Walton County Sheriff’s Office arresting Dr. Thomas Shaknovsky on April 13.
Officers with the Walton County Sheriff’s Office arresting Dr. Thomas Shaknovsky on April 13.

media:content.media:credit: Anuj Shrestha for NBC News; Leila Register
media:content.media:text: The liver, the body's largest internal organ, and the spleen are both under the rib cage.
media:content.media:description: The liver, the body's largest internal organ, and the spleen are both under the rib cage.

item.title: Florida surgeon who removed wrong organ says he is ‘forever traumatized’ by patient’s death

item.description: A possible suicide note from convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein has now been publicly revealed for the first time. The handwritten text spans roughly half a page and has not been authenticated by NBC News. It was not previously released in the so-called Epstein files by the Department of Justice and only became public after Epstein’s former cellmate, Nicholas Tartaglione, said he found it in a book in the cell they shared in 2019 as Epstein awaited trial on sex trafficking charges. NBC’s

media:content.media:title: Alleged Suicide Note From Jeffrey Epstein is Released by Judge
media:content.media:description: A possible suicide note from convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein has now been publicly revealed for the first time. The handwritten text spans roughly half a page and has not been authenticated by NBC News. It was not previously released in the so-called Epstein files by the Department of Justice and only became public after Epstein’s former cellmate, Nicholas Tartaglione, said he found it in a book in the cell they shared in 2019 as Epstein awaited trial on sex trafficking charges. NBC’s Laura Jarrett reports TODAY.

item.title: Alleged Suicide Note From Jeffrey Epstein is Released by Judge

item.dc:creator: Jon Schuppe
Mackenzie Mays, Bloomberg Law
item.description: The proposed legislation follows an investigation by Bloomberg Law and NBC News that found mistreatment of pregnant women in local jails.

media:content.media:description: Rep. Sydney Kamlager-Dove, D-Calif., during a House Judiciary Committee hearing in the Rayburn House Office Building on March 4, 2026.

media:content.media:text: Kelsey Love, also featured in the investigation, gave birth alone in jail after pleading for help. Her son survived.
media:content.media:description: Kelsey Love, also featured in the investigation, gave birth alone in jail after pleading for help. Her son survived.

media:content.media:text: Bloomberg Law and NBC News' investigation highlighted the jailing of Chasity Congious, who gave birth by herself in a cell to a baby who died after strangling on the umbilical cord.
media:content.media:description: Bloomberg Law and NBC News' investigation highlighted the jailing of Chasity Congious, who gave birth by herself in a cell to a baby who died after strangling on the umbilical cord.

media:content.media:text: Lauren Kent lost a baby to miscarriage. She now has two sons and hopes her story will help improve prenatal care in America's jails.
media:content.media:description: Lauren Kent lost a baby to miscarriage. She now has two sons and hopes her story will help improve prenatal care in America's jails.

item.title: A new federal bill aims to protect pregnant women held in prisons and jails

item.description: In our series “The Cost of Denial,” NBC News’ Erin McLaughlin reports on the problems facing military families with their healthcare coverage. One veteran tells us he was denied coverage for post-cancer treatment because the health plan’s administrator mistakenly believed he had other health insurance.

media:content.media:title: Military families call out healthcare company
media:content.media:description: In our series “The Cost of Denial,” NBC News’ Erin McLaughlin reports on the problems facing military families with their healthcare coverage. One veteran tells us he was denied coverage for post-cancer treatment because the health plan’s administrator mistakenly believed he had other health insurance.

item.title: Military families call out healthcare company
